demo: small non-circular TRANSFER experiment (NOT a fine-grained calibration curve) — claimed confidence grounded on the SEEN task, success measured on an UNSEEN variant task
honesty: Confidence grounded on the SEEN task (test_hidden); success measured on a DIFFERENT UNSEEN variant (test_unseen) — genuine out-of-sample transfer, not circular. Beta(1,1)+outcomes = live gate math. HONEST LIMITS: only two confidence levels (~0.1/~0.9) → COARSE high/low separation, NOT a fine calibration curve over [0,1]. Effective ~10 class-points, not 80. 3/5 classes Qwen solves without memory (no contrast). pagination_leak claimed 0.9 but transferred 0/8 — a REAL miscalibration we SURFACE; Brier 0.09/ECE 0.04 are dominated by it and diluted by easy classes (see contrast_only). Beta convergence shown separately+synthetically in tests/test_calibration.py — not merged.
effective_units: ≈10 class-points (8 samples per group share one confidence), not 80

contrast_only.note: memory-signal classes only: claim ~0.9 but transfer ~0.5 (pagination_leak claims 0.9, transfers 0/8). The aggregate hides this; surfaced on purpose.
